name: Efficient School TRNSPRTN
districts_or_customers: 15+ MN districts incl. Mounds View, Robbinsdale, Eden Prairie, Metro Deaf School, Northwest Suburban Integration District
recent_news: Operates as minority-owned SPED/alternative student transport provider serving Twin Cities metro; no recent M&A news identified.
fit_notes: Small minority-owned MN SPED/alt-transport operator; founder still active and relatively young (low succession signal), but could fit Summit's MN footprint as a niche SPED/McKinney-Vento bolt-on. Modest scale (~$1.4M revenue).

name: Robinson Transport Inc
employees_est: 250-400 (est.; revenue/contract scale implies materially more than the 40 listed)
districts_or_customers: 30+ contracts across Southern IL and SE MO incl. Massac County Unit 1, Joppa-Maple Grove; offices in Metropolis IL, Harrisburg IL, and Cape Girardeau MO
recent_news: Active recruiting drive in 2023 with $1,000 sign-on bonus for drivers; family-owned with husband-and-wife founders having 25 and 50 years of industry experience respectively.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: family-owned, 300+ buses, 30+ contracts across IL/MO, founders have 25/50 years experience (clear succession signal). Multi-state Southern IL/SE MO footprint complements Summit's Midwest platform. Revenue likely materially higher than the $1.2M listed (scale/data appears stale).

name: Positive Connections
owner_or_parent: Student Transportation of America (STA / Ridesta) - acquired May 1, 2006
fleet_size_est: ~198 vehicles (per FMCSA at acquisition; 400 vehicles added at original deal incl. multi-state)
districts_or_customers: Chicago-area special needs and school routes; operates under STA's Midwest platform from Markham IL (2200/2320 W 167th/159th St); originally added six locations across IL and MN to STA
recent_news: Acquired by Student Transportation of America (now Ridesta) on May 1, 2006 in STA's largest post-IPO deal; added ~$18M revenue and 400 vehicles, establishing STA's Midwest platform. Continues operating under the Positive Connections name within STA.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Owned by Student Transportation of America (Ridesta) since 2006 - a major strategic competitor to Summit. Operates as STA's Midwest SPED platform brand.

name: S & L Transportation
principals: Linda Sauerzopf (President, L&S Transportation Inc, Wind Gap PA)
districts_or_customers: Unverified; historical PA charter/paratransit operator out of Wind Gap, Northampton County
recent_news: Record appears to conflate two entities: (1) L&S Transportation Inc at 104 Hoffman Rd, Wind Gap PA (Linda Sauerzopf) - FMCSA USDOT 953640 shows operating status 'Inactive'; and (2) S&L Transportation in Southfield/Detroit MI (NEMT) - BBB shows 'no longer in business.' Neither appears to be an active independent school bus operator.
fit_notes: NOT a viable tuck-in: data record appears to conflate a PA charter operator (L&S Transportation Inc, Wind Gap - FMCSA 'Inactive') and a closed MI NEMT outfit (S&L Transportation, Southfield - BBB 'no longer in business'). No evidence of active school bus contracting.

name: M V Transportation
owner_or_parent: MV Transportation, Inc. (founder-owned by Alex & Feysan Lodde; largest privately-owned transit/paratransit company in North America)
principals: Alex & Feysan Lodde (founders/owners, parent); Susan Macdonell (Morro Bay GM)
fleet_size_est: ~3 vehicles at Morro Bay site (1 Runabout + 2 Molly Trolleys); parent operates thousands of vehicles nationally
service_types: (some school nationally, not at Morro Bay)
districts_or_customers: City of Morro Bay public transit contract (Morro Bay Transit / Molly Trolley); contract being transitioned to SLO Regional Transit Authority (RTA)
recent_news: Parent MV Transportation celebrated 50th anniversary Aug 2025; still founder-owned. Morro Bay contract being handed off to SLO Regional Transit Authority as City exits direct transit involvement.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. This is a branch of MV Transportation, Inc., the largest privately-owned transit/paratransit company in North America (founder-owned). The Morro Bay site is a tiny municipal transit contract (not school bus) that is being transitioned away to the SLO RTA.
